rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DAVID FRANK PHILLIPS II
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/29/25 (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proof of service was filed concurrently with the "Declaration" filed on 8/18/25. (2) Not all receipts for the preliminary distributions ordered on 11/23/22 have been filed yet, and bank statements confirming that the estate funds are in FDIC insured accounts have not been filed yet. (3) The incompletely dated receipt signed by Jon Sneddon was not for the distribution ordered on 11/23/22. (4) The beginning value as of 1/1/22 should be $2,812,331.85, which was the ending balance of the executor's prior accounting. (5) The accounting should then reflect what happened with the vehicle appraised at $800. (6) Please file proof that the intended recipient of the automobile declined the gift. Was Arianna not in a position to receive the gift after Noah declined it, or did the executor give her the option to receive it? (7) Please file a receipt or other proof of transfer of the vehicle to the "person employed to help prepare the real property for sale." (A personal representative has the power to abandon tangible personal property AFTER giving Notice of Proposed Action. [Probate Code Section 10513.]) (8) Page 2 of 2 of the Summary of Account that is Exhibit C to the attorney's declaration e-filed 6/25/25 shows Total Mutual Funds / Exchange Traded Funds as having a CV of $538,304.52 and FMV of $673,209.35. These figures do not appear to be correct. (9) The 11/23/22 order for preliminary distribution authorized Executor to distribute $2,000 to Christopher Phillips as an honorarium to assist in preparing the heraldic book, and Rabbi Ungar apparently assigned his devise to Christopher (the unpublished book, $15,000 for costs to publish the book, and $5,000 + interest from 3/26/21 to the date of distribution to provide free copies of the book), but the accounting shows $10,000 disbursed to Rabbi Ungar as an "honorarium," $5,000 to Chrisopher Philips for the book publication, and another $18,000 to Christopher for the book distribution. (10) Christopher was reimbursed $10,393.91 on 5/11/23, when the 11/23/22 order permitted him to be reimbursed $9,276.88. (11) For what estate expenses was Adam Phillips reimbursed $3,010.36? Deadline is 10/15/25 to provide the assigned examiner courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues. (Issues should be addressed by just filing the proper papers (a supplement or an amendment to the amended petition, or the correct proof of service, or the original bank statements, etc.), not by "responding" to "Examiner Notes," which are not filed for the record.) Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (After all procedural issues are addressed, an appearance will be required for the fees requested and because the rather large ($11,343.41) adjustment to the accounting to make it balance.)

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE JEWEL DENT REVOCABLE LIVING TRUST DATED 8/30/17
rulings.Calendar Matter: Verified Petition For Orders Suspension And Removal Of Kenneth Lewis As Successor Trustee And Appointment Of Patrick Johnson As Successor Trustee; Requiring Respondent To Provide An Accounting In Accordance With Probate Code 1060 Et Seq.; Compelling The Return Of Property Wrongfully Taken, Recovery Of Compensatory Damages And Double Damages Pursuant To Probate Code 850-859; Requiring Respondent To Hold Settlor'S Property In A Constructive Trust For The Benefit Of The Beneficiaries
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/21/2025. Reasons necessitating a continuance: 1) Notice is insufficient. All notices must include language about remote appearances (San Francisco Local Rule 14.1.C.6). Form DE-115 is required for petitions seeking damages under Probate Code Section 859. Notice was provided to Cheryl Monique Worley on August 12, 2025, just 22 days prior to the hearing (Probate Code Sections 175203). 2) A notice mailed to a person in care of another person in insufficient unless the person entitled to notice is an adult and has directed the party giving notice in writing to send the notice in care of the second person (CRC 7.51(a)(2)). Please provide direct notice to Kenneth Lewis. 3) Please provide the date of death of Mary Harris and identify all her children. 4) Please provide the date of death of Jewell Hall and identify all her children. 5) Please identify all living children of Jewell Freeman. 6) Please identify all deceased children of Jewell Freeman and their living children (grandchildren of Jewell Freeman). Please address the above issues and provide courtesy copies of all further filings to this examiner no later than 10/07/2025, to avoid further continuance.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CECIE WEST
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/21/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: Still no courtesy copies have been received by the assigned examiner, which can delay or hinder full review. (1) If the decedent died in 1975, all of her six children who are now deceased POST-deceased her. Were estates not opened for any of them other than Beverly West? Who are their successors-in-interest? (2) The petitioner - the person named in item 2 of the petition - is missing from item 8 of the petition. (3) If the petitioner is (presumably) the son of Myra Maximo, have the surviving children declined to serve or nominated him? (4) Notice must be given to the successors-in-interest of the post-deceased children, including the special administrator of the estate of Beverly West. Please provide the assigned examiner courtesy copies of the petition and any papers filed to address these issues by 10/7/25. Courtesy copies are file stamped, single-sided, hard copies. [LRSF 14.7 B. and C.] Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LOIS LEE GUILFO
rulings.Calendar Matter: REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/28/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been lodged or received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) No proof of service of the notice of this hearing has been filed. (3) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (4) The report does not appear to meet all the requirements of LRSF 14.35 J. What actions has the administrator taken re the creditor claims? (5) There does not appear to be an exhibit attached to the petition. Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of the petition and any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 10/14/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (Item 4 of an I&A should NOT be completed when a probate referee HAS BEEN appointed.)

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE IN RE: RICHARD YOUNG 2025 TRUST TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For 1) Trust Contest; 2) Removal Of Trustee; 3) Invalid Donative Transfer Pursuant To Probate Code 21360 Et Seq And 21380 Et Seq 4) Disinheri
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/21/2025. Reasons necessitating a continuance: 1) Notice is insufficient. Personal service of the trustee is required (Probate Code Section 851(a)(1)). No names or addresses appear on the proof of service attached to the DE-115 filed July 9, 2025. 2) No proposed order has been received or reviewed by the court. Any party lodging a proposed order in a contested matter must file proof of service of the proposed order on all parties entitled to notice of the petition (San Francisco Local Rule 14.7.A). Please address the above issues and provide courtesy copies of all further filings to this examiner no later than 10/07/2025, to avoid further continuance.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RICHARD YOUNG
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/21/2025. Reasons necessitating a continuance: 1) A proposed nonresident personal representative will be required to post a bond to protect California creditors, even if the will waives, or all beneficiaries waive, bond. A declaration or attachment to the petition setting forth in detail the anticipated liabilities of the decedent and claims against the estate will be used by the Court to determine the amount of the bond, but in no event will the bond be less than $20,000 (San Francisco Local Rule 14.16.F). 2) Item 6f is checked although it appears that Item 6b applies. 3) When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor through whom they take (or would have taken) must be named, the relationship to the decedent stated, and the date of death specified (San Francisco Local Rule 14.18.C.3). 4) No Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Form DE-147S) has been filed (San Francisco Local Rule 14.18.E). 5) No proposed order has been received or lodged with the court. (San Francisco Local Rule 14.7.A). Please address the above issues and provide courtesy copies of all further filings to this examiner no later than 10/07/2025, to avoid further continuance.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RICHARD YOUNG
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/21/2025. Reasons necessitating a continuance: 1) Petition is incomplete: In item 5a, box 3 or 4 must be checked, box 5 or 6 must be checked, and box 7 or 8 must be checked. Item 6f is checked although it appears that Item 6b applies. 2) Tina Dykas's address must be listed in Item 8. 3) When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor through whom they take (or would have taken) must be named, the relationship to the decedent stated, and the date of death specified (San Francisco Local Rule 14.18.C.3). 4) No Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Form DE-147S) has been filed (San Francisco Local Rule 14.18.E). 5) All notices must include language about remote appearances (San Francisco Local Rule 14.1.C.6). 6) Form DE-140 is the mandatory form for a proposed order. No DE-140 has been received or lodged with the court. (San Francisco Local Rule 14.7.A). Please address the above issues and provide courtesy copies of all further filings to this examiner no later than 10/07/2025, to avoid further continuance.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE AMENDED AND RESTATED LYDIAH. VARGAS 2008 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For: 1 Accounting; 2 Copies Of Estate Planning Instruments; 3 Suspension, Removal,And Replacement Of Trustee; 4 Recovery Of Trust Property
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Parties to make a first appearance. Note: the property subject to the 850 petition has not been sufficiently described. Remote appearances may be made. Please review Local Rule 14.1. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. Meeting ID: 160 225 4765 Password: 514879 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above.

rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/16/25 to be reviewed with competing petition.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF HENRY ALAN BURNS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. Status hearing on filing of petition for final distribution or petition for approval on report of status of administration will be set for 03/16/2027.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF KATHRYN A. MURRAY
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required to admit a lost will. Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SIMEONA GONZALES
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. Status hearing for filing of a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report on status of administration is set for 03/02/2027.
